Kathryn Wong'S Petition For Instructions And For Judgment And Order To Determine Whether Petitioners Helen Wong-Joe, Jessica Wong, And Brandon Wong Have Any Interest In The Chung Hop Wong And King Tsai Lau Wong Revocable Living Trust Dated September 21, 2009, Amended September 26, 2009 Due To Violation Of The Trusts No Contest Clause, Arguing That Its Time Barred Nature Rendered It A Direct Contest Without Probable Cause
Off Calendar Jan-14-2026 Continued To Apr-22-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mark Malachowski for beneficiaries Helen Wong-Joe, Jessica Wong, and Brandon Wong.
Daniel Hales for trustee Kathryn Wong (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on: 1) First Amended Petition for Compensatory Damages, Double Damages, Cancellation of Written Instrument, Breach of Fiduciary Duties, etc.; and 2) Kathryn Wong's Petition for Instructions and to Determine Whether Helen Wong-Joe, Jessica Wong, and Brandon Wong Have Any Interest in the Trust Due to Violation of No Contest Clause.

The Court inquired regarding the status of discovery and whether the matter might be appropriate for mediation. Mr. Malachowski reported that discovery was ongoing, including efforts to obtain bank records, and stated that mediation might be appropriate following further discovery. Mr. Hales likewise reported that discovery was ongoing and argued that the beneficiaries' petition constituted an untimely trust contest barred by the no contest clause.

Mr. Malachowski responded that the beneficiaries' petition sought to enforce the trust and alleged breach of fiduciary duty, undue influence, and lack of capacity relating to the June 2022 grant deed, and therefore did not constitute a direct trust contest.

The Court stated it was not prepared to dismiss the beneficiaries' petition at this time and that the matter was not ready to be decided. The Court stated that, if the matter did not resolve, it may discuss the filing of a joint case management statement and next steps toward setting an evidentiary hearing at the next calling of the case..

The Court continued all matters to April 22, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, and added status on mediation.

Off Calendar Oct-08-2025 Continued To Dec-17-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Kathryn Wong'S Petition For Instructions And For Judgment And Order To Determine Whether Petitioners Helen Wong-Joe, Jessica Wong, And Brandon Wong Have Any Interest In The Chung Hop Wong And King Tsai Lau Wong Revocable Living Trust Dated September 21, 2009, Amended September 26, 2009 Due To Violation Of The Trusts No Contest Clause, Arguing That Its Time Barred Nature Rendered It A Direct Contest Without Probable Cause
Off Calendar Oct-08-2025 Continued To Dec-17-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mark Malachowski for petitioners Helen Wong-Joe, Jessica Wong, and Brandon Wong.
Daniel Hales for trustee Kathryn Wong (via Zoom).

Counsel reported that discovery is still ongoing, with motions to quash subpoenas filed by both sides set for hearing on December 4, 2025.

Mr. Hales argued that petitioners' September 11, 2023 petition constitutes a late-filed contest brought more than 120 days after notice under Probate Code Section 16061.7 and is therefore barred. He requested dismissal under Probate Code Section 21311. The Court declined to dismiss the petition and noted that the issues raised were not ready to be resolved on the papers and required further development.

Mr. Malachowski argued in response that the petition alleges breach of fiduciary duty and undue influence relating to a June 2022 grant deed transferring real property from the trustor to respondent five days before his death, and therefore falls within the three-year statute of limitations for breach-of-trust claims. He also argued that the decedent lacked capacity and that the transfer resulted from undue influence exerted by respondent, who allegedly acted in multiple fiduciary capacities including agent and translator.

The Court discussed the option of informal discovery conferences with the department as well as the Court's pro bono mediation program for matters valued under $3 million.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to December 17, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to allow time for discovery and for counsel to consider mediation.
